last_assistant_message:
All acceptance criteria are met. Here is the report:

DONE: Wave 4 Track D documentation complete — 6 doc files written in astramemory-local, plugin README updated with Local backend section.

Files:
- `C:\work\mega\astramemory-local\README.md` — full v1 README (replaced stub)
- `C:\work\mega\astramemory-local\CHANGELOG.md` — Keep-a-Changelog v0.1.0 release notes
- `C:\work\mega\astramemory-local\docs\migration-from-saas.md` — 7-step executable migration guide
- `C:\work\mega\astramemory-local\docs\configuration.md` — full config.yaml reference with all fields and defaults
- `C:\work\mega\astramemory-local\docs\providers.md` — Ollama and Azure OpenAI setup with verification commands
- `C:\work\mega\astramemory-local\docs\troubleshooting.md` — 7 common issues with root-cause + fix
- `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\README.md` — `## Local backend (optional)` section added after the existing intro, no other changes

Commits: `77b2ab3` in astramemory-local, `1a4d1b3` in astramemory-plugin.
Tag: `wave-4-D-docs` confirmed in astramemory-local (existing tags wave-1 through wave-4-B also present).
Risks: CHANGELOG section placement note — the spec says "What's in it" section but the plugin README has that content inline before "Pair a workstation"; the new section was inserted immediately before "Pair a workstation" which is the first standalone `##` after the hooks/MCP intro content, matching the spec intent. No external dependencies for diagrams — all ASCII only.
